verses.kjv: O the depth of the riches both of the wisdom and knowledge of God! how unsearchable are his judgments, and his ways past finding out!
verses.bsb: O, the depth of the riches of the wisdom and knowledge of God! How unsearchable are His judgments, and untraceable His ways!

verses.kjv: For who hath known the mind of the Lord? or who hath been his counseller?
verses.bsb: “Who has known the mind of the Lord? Or who has been His counselor?”

verses.kjv: Or who hath first given to him, and it shall be recompensed unto him again?
verses.bsb: “Who has first given to God, that God should repay him?”

verses.kjv: For of him, and through him, and to him, are all things: to whom be glory for ever. Amen.
verses.bsb: For from Him and through Him and to Him are all things. To Him be the glory forever! Amen.

verses.kjv: I BESEECH you therefore, brethren, by the mercies of God, that ye present your bodies a living sacrifice, holy, acceptable unto God, which is your reasonable service.
verses.bsb: Therefore I urge you, brothers, on account of God’s mercy, to offer your bodies as living sacrifices, holy and pleasing to God, which is your spiritual service of worship.

verses.kjv: And be not conformed to this world: but be ye transformed by the renewing of your mind, that ye may prove what is that good, and acceptable, and perfect, will of God.
verses.bsb: Do not be conformed to this world, but be transformed by the renewing of your mind. Then you will be able to test and approve what is the good, pleasing, and perfect will of God.

verses.kjv: For I say, through the grace given unto me, to every man that is among you, not to think of himself more highly than he ought to think; but to think soberly, according as God hath dealt to every man the measure of faith.
verses.bsb: For by the grace given me I say to every one of you: Do not think of yourself more highly than you ought, but think of yourself with sober judgment, according to the measure of faith God has given you.

verses.kjv: For as we have many members in one body, and all members have not the same office:
verses.bsb: Just as each of us has one body with many members, and not all members have the same function,

verses.kjv: So we, being many, are one body in Christ, and every one members one of another.
verses.bsb: so in Christ we who are many are one body, and each member belongs to one another.

verses.kjv: Having then gifts differing according to the grace that is given to us, whether prophecy, let us prophesy according to the proportion of faith;
verses.bsb: We have different gifts according to the grace given us. If one’s gift is prophecy, let him use it in proportion to his faith;

verses.kjv: Or ministry, let us wait on our ministering: or he that teacheth, on teaching;
verses.bsb: if it is serving, let him serve; if it is teaching, let him teach;

verses.kjv: Or he that exhorteth, on exhortation: he that giveth, let him do it with simplicity; he that ruleth, with diligence; he that sheweth mercy, with cheerfulness.
verses.bsb: if it is encouraging, let him encourage; if it is giving, let him give generously; if it is leading, let him lead with diligence; if it is showing mercy, let him do it cheerfully.

verses.kjv: Let love be without dissimulation. Abhor that which is evil; cleave to that which is good.
verses.bsb: Love must be sincere. Detest what is evil; cling to what is good.

verses.kjv: Be kindly affectioned one to another with brotherly love; in honour preferring one another;
verses.bsb: Be devoted to one another in brotherly love. Outdo yourselves in honoring one another.

verses.kjv: Not slothful in business; fervent in spirit; serving the Lord;
verses.bsb: Do not let your zeal subside; keep your spiritual fervor, serving the Lord.

verses.kjv: Rejoicing in hope; patient in tribulation; continuing instant in prayer;
verses.bsb: Be joyful in hope, patient in affliction, persistent in prayer.

verses.kjv: Distributing to the necessity of saints; given to hospitality.
verses.bsb: Share with the saints who are in need. Practice hospitality.

verses.kjv: Bless them which persecute you: bless, and curse not.
verses.bsb: Bless those who persecute you. Bless and do not curse.

verses.kjv: Rejoice with them that do rejoice, and weep with them that weep.
verses.bsb: Rejoice with those who rejoice; weep with those who weep.

verses.kjv: Be of the same mind one toward another. Mind not high things, but condescend to men of low estate. Be not wise in your own conceits.
verses.bsb: Live in harmony with one another. Do not be proud, but enjoy the company of the lowly. Do not be conceited.

verses.kjv: Recompense to no man evil for evil. Provide things honest in the sight of all men.
verses.bsb: Do not repay anyone evil for evil. Carefully consider what is right in the eyes of everybody.

verses.kjv: If it be possible, as much as lieth in you, live peaceably with all men.
verses.bsb: If it is possible on your part, live at peace with everyone.

verses.kjv: Dearly beloved, avenge not yourselves, but rather give place unto wrath: for it is written, Vengeance is mine; I will repay, saith the Lord.
verses.bsb: Do not avenge yourselves, beloved, but leave room for God’s wrath. For it is written: “Vengeance is Mine; I will repay, says the Lord.”

verses.kjv: Therefore if thine enemy hunger, feed him; if he thirst, give him drink: for in so doing thou shalt heap coals of fire on his head.
verses.bsb: On the contrary,  “If your enemy is hungry, feed him; if he is thirsty, give him a drink. For in so doing, you will heap burning coals on his head.”

verses.kjv: Be not overcome of evil, but overcome evil with good.
verses.bsb: Do not be overcome by evil, but overcome evil with good.

verses.kjv: LET every soul be subject unto the higher powers. For there is no power but of God: the powers that be are ordained of God.
verses.bsb: Everyone must submit himself to the governing authorities, for there is no authority except that which is from God. The authorities that exist have been appointed by God.

verses.kjv: Whosoever therefore resisteth the power, resisteth the ordinance of God: and they that resist shall receive to themselves damnation.
verses.bsb: Consequently, whoever resists authority is opposing what God has set in place, and those who do so will bring judgment on themselves.

verses.kjv: For rulers are not a terror to good works, but to the evil. Wilt thou then not be afraid of the power? do that which is good, and thou shalt have praise of the same:
verses.bsb: For rulers are not a terror to good conduct, but to bad. Do you want to be unafraid of the one in authority? Then do what is right, and you will have his approval.

verses.kjv: For he is the minister of God to thee for good. But if thou do that which is evil, be afraid; for he beareth not the sword in vain: for he is the minister of God, a revenger to execute wrath upon him that doeth evil.
verses.bsb: For he is God’s servant for your good. But if you do wrong, be afraid, for he does not carry the sword in vain. He is God’s servant, an agent of retribution to the wrongdoer.

verses.kjv: Wherefore ye must needs be subject, not only for wrath, but also for conscience sake.
verses.bsb: Therefore it is necessary to submit to authority, not only to avoid punishment, but also as a matter of conscience.

verses.kjv: For for this cause pay ye tribute also: for they are God’s ministers, attending continually upon this very thing.
verses.bsb: This is also why you pay taxes. For the authorities are God’s servants, who devote themselves to their work.

verses.kjv: Render therefore to all their dues: tribute to whom tribute is due; custom to whom custom; fear to whom fear; honour to whom honour.
verses.bsb: Pay everyone what you owe him: taxes to whom taxes are due, revenue to whom revenue is due, respect to whom respect is due, honor to whom honor is due.

verses.kjv: Owe no man any thing, but to love one another: for he that loveth another hath fulfilled the law.
verses.bsb: Be indebted to no one, except to one another in love. For he who loves his neighbor has fulfilled the law.

verses.kjv: For this, Thou shalt not commit adultery, Thou shalt not kill, Thou shalt not steal, Thou shalt not bear false witness, Thou shalt not covet; and if there be any other commandment, it is briefly comprehended in this saying, namely, Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself.
verses.bsb: The commandments “Do not commit adultery,” “Do not murder,” “Do not steal,” “Do not covet,” and any other commandments, are summed up in this one decree: “Love your neighbor as yourself.”

verses.kjv: Love worketh no ill to his neighbour: therefore love is the fulfilling of the law.
verses.bsb: Love does no wrong to its neighbor. Therefore love is the fulfillment of the law.

verses.kjv: And that, knowing the time, that now it is high time to awake out of sleep: for now is our salvation nearer than when we believed.
verses.bsb: And do this, understanding the occasion. The hour has come for you to wake up from your slumber, for our salvation is nearer now than when we first believed.

verses.kjv: The night is far spent, the day is at hand: let us therefore cast off the works of darkness, and let us put on the armour of light.
verses.bsb: The night is nearly over; the day has drawn near. So let us lay aside the deeds of darkness and put on the armor of light.

verses.kjv: Let us walk honestly, as in the day; not in rioting and drunkenness, not in chambering and wantonness, not in strife and envying.
verses.bsb: Let us behave decently, as in the daytime, not in carousing and drunkenness, not in sexual immorality and debauchery, not in dissension and jealousy.

verses.kjv: But put ye on the Lord Jesus Christ, and make not provision for the flesh, to fulfil the lusts thereof.
verses.bsb: Instead, clothe yourselves with the Lord Jesus Christ, and make no provision for the desires of the flesh.

verses.kjv: HIM that is weak in the faith receive ye, but not to doubtful disputations.
verses.bsb: Accept him whose faith is weak, without passing judgment on his opinions.

verses.kjv: For one believeth that he may eat all things: another, who is weak, eateth herbs.
verses.bsb: For one person has faith to eat all things, while another, who is weak, eats only vegetables.

verses.kjv: Let not him that eateth despise him that eateth not; and let not him which eateth not judge him that eateth: for God hath received him.
verses.bsb: The one who eats everything must not belittle the one who does not, and the one who does not eat everything must not judge the one who does, for God has accepted him.

verses.kjv: Who art thou that judgest another man’s servant? to his own master he standeth or falleth. Yea, he shall be holden up: for God is able to make him stand.
verses.bsb: Who are you to judge someone else’s servant? To his own master he stands or falls. And he will stand, for the Lord is able to make him stand.

verses.kjv: One man esteemeth one day above another: another esteemeth every day alike. Let every man be fully persuaded in his own mind.
verses.bsb: One person regards a certain day above the others, while someone else considers every day alike. Each one should be fully convinced in his own mind.

verses.kjv: He that regardeth the day, regardeth it unto the Lord; and he that regardeth not the day, to the Lord he doth not regard it. He that eateth, eateth to the Lord, for he giveth God thanks; and he that eateth not, to the Lord he eateth not, and giveth God thanks.
verses.bsb: He who observes a special day does so to the Lord; he who eats does so to the Lord, for he gives thanks to God; and he who abstains does so to the Lord and gives thanks to God.

verses.kjv: For none of us liveth to himself, and no man dieth to himself.
verses.bsb: For none of us lives to himself alone, and none of us dies to himself alone.

verses.kjv: For whether we live, we live unto the Lord; and whether we die, we die unto the Lord: whether we live therefore, or die, we are the Lord’s.
verses.bsb: If we live, we live to the Lord, and if we die, we die to the Lord. So whether we live or die, we belong to the Lord.

verses.kjv: For to this end Christ both died, and rose, and revived, that he might be Lord both of the dead and living.
verses.bsb: For this reason Christ died and returned to life, that He might be the Lord of both the dead and the living.

verses.kjv: But why dost thou judge thy brother? or why dost thou set at nought thy brother? for we shall all stand before the judgment seat of Christ.
verses.bsb: Why, then, do you judge your brother? Or why do you belittle your brother? For we will all stand before God’s judgment seat.

verses.kjv: For it is written, As I live, saith the Lord, every knee shall bow to me, and every tongue shall confess to God.
verses.bsb: It is written:  “As surely as I live, says the Lord, every knee will bow before Me; every tongue will confess to God.”

verses.kjv: So then every one of us shall give account of himself to God.
verses.bsb: So then, each of us will give an account of himself to God.

verses.kjv: Let us not therefore judge one another any more: but judge this rather, that no man put a stumblingblock or an occasion to fall in his brother’s way.
verses.bsb: Therefore let us stop judging one another. Instead, make up your mind not to put any stumbling block or obstacle in your brother’s way.

verses.kjv: I know, and am persuaded by the Lord Jesus, that there is nothing unclean of itself: but to him that esteemeth any thing to be unclean, to him it is unclean.
verses.bsb: I am convinced and fully persuaded in the Lord Jesus that nothing is unclean in itself. But if anyone regards something as unclean, then for him it is unclean.

verses.kjv: But if thy brother be grieved with thy meat, now walkest thou not charitably. Destroy not him with thy meat, for whom Christ died.
verses.bsb: If your brother is distressed by what you eat, you are no longer acting in love. Do not by your eating destroy your brother, for whom Christ died.

verses.kjv: Let not then your good be evil spoken of:
verses.bsb: Do not allow what you consider good, then, to be spoken of as evil.

verses.kjv: For the kingdom of God is not meat and drink; but righteousness, and peace, and joy in the Holy Ghost.
verses.bsb: For the kingdom of God is not a matter of eating and drinking, but of righteousness, peace, and joy in the Holy Spirit.

verses.kjv: For he that in these things serveth Christ is acceptable to God, and approved of men.
verses.bsb: For whoever serves Christ in this way is pleasing to God and approved by men.

verses.kjv: Let us therefore follow after the things which make for peace, and things wherewith one may edify another.
verses.bsb: So then, let us pursue what leads to peace and to mutual edification.

verses.kjv: For meat destroy not the work of God. All things indeed are pure; but it is evil for that man who eateth with offence.
verses.bsb: Do not destroy the work of God for the sake of food. All food is clean, but it is wrong for a man to let his eating be a stumbling block.

verses.kjv: It is good neither to eat flesh, nor to drink wine, nor any thing whereby thy brother stumbleth, or is offended, or is made weak.
verses.bsb: It is better not to eat meat or drink wine or to do anything to cause your brother to stumble.

verses.kjv: Hast thou faith? have it to thyself before God. Happy is he that condemneth not himself in that thing which he alloweth.
verses.bsb: Keep your belief about such matters between yourself and God. Blessed is the one who does not condemn himself by what he approves.

verses.kjv: And he that doubteth is damned if he eat, because he eateth not of faith: for whatsoever is not of faith is sin.
verses.bsb: But the one who has doubts is condemned if he eats, because his eating is not from faith; and everything that is not from faith is sin.

verses.kjv: WE then that are strong ought to bear the infirmities of the weak, and not to please ourselves.
verses.bsb: We who are strong ought to bear with the shortcomings of the weak and not to please ourselves.

verses.kjv: Let every one of us please his neighbour for his good to edification.
verses.bsb: Each of us should please his neighbor for his good, to build him up.

verses.kjv: For even Christ pleased not himself; but, as it is written, The reproaches of them that reproached thee fell on me.
verses.bsb: For even Christ did not please Himself, but as it is written: “The insults of those who insult You have fallen on Me.”

verses.kjv: For whatsoever things were written aforetime were written for our learning, that we through patience and comfort of the scriptures might have hope.
verses.bsb: For everything that was written in the past was written for our instruction, so that through endurance and the encouragement of the Scriptures, we might have hope.

verses.kjv: Now the God of patience and consolation grant you to be likeminded one toward another according to Christ Jesus:
verses.bsb: Now may the God who gives endurance and encouragement grant you harmony with one another in Christ Jesus,

verses.kjv: That ye may with one mind and one mouth glorify God, even the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.
verses.bsb: so that with one mind and one voice you may glorify the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.kjv: Wherefore receive ye one another, as Christ also received us to the glory of God.
verses.bsb: Accept one another, then, just as Christ accepted you, in order to bring glory to God.

verses.kjv: Now I say that Jesus Christ was a minister of the circumcision for the truth of God, to confirm the promises made unto the fathers:
verses.bsb: For I tell you that Christ has become a servant of the circumcised on behalf of God’s truth, to confirm the promises made to the patriarchs,

verses.kjv: And that the Gentiles might glorify God for his mercy; as it is written, For this cause I will confess to thee among the Gentiles, and sing unto thy name.
verses.bsb: so that the Gentiles may glorify God for His mercy. As it is written:  “Therefore I will praise You among the Gentiles; I will sing hymns to Your name.”

verses.kjv: And again he saith, Rejoice, ye Gentiles, with his people.
verses.bsb: Again, it says:  “Rejoice, O Gentiles, with His people.”

verses.kjv: And again, Praise the Lord, all ye Gentiles; and laud him, all ye people.
verses.bsb: And again:  “Praise the Lord, all you Gentiles, and extol Him, all you peoples.”

verses.kjv: And again, Esaias saith, There shall be a root of Jesse, and he that shall rise to reign over the Gentiles; in him shall the Gentiles trust.
verses.bsb: And once more, Isaiah says:  “The Root of Jesse will appear, One who will arise to rule over the Gentiles; in Him the Gentiles will put their hope.”

verses.kjv: Now the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in believing, that ye may abound in hope, through the power of the Holy Ghost.
verses.bsb: Now may the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace as you believe in Him, so that you may overflow with hope by the power of the Holy Spirit.

verses.kjv: And I myself also am persuaded of you, my brethren, that ye also are full of goodness, filled with all knowledge, able also to admonish one another.
verses.bsb: I myself am convinced, my brothers, that you yourselves are full of goodness, brimming with knowledge, and able to instruct one another.

verses.kjv: Nevertheless, brethren, I have written the more boldly unto you in some sort, as putting you in mind, because of the grace that is given to me of God,
verses.bsb: However, I have written you a bold reminder on some points, because of the grace God has given me

verses.kjv: That I should be the minister of Jesus Christ to the Gentiles, ministering the gospel of God, that the offering up of the Gentiles might be acceptable, being sanctified by the Holy Ghost.
verses.bsb: to be a minister of Christ Jesus to the Gentiles in the priestly service of the gospel of God, so that the Gentiles might become an offering acceptable to God, sanctified by the Holy Spirit.

verses.kjv: I have therefore whereof I may glory through Jesus Christ in those things which pertain to God.
verses.bsb: Therefore I exult in Christ Jesus in my service to God.

verses.kjv: For I will not dare to speak of any of those things which Christ hath not wrought by me, to make the Gentiles obedient, by word and deed,
verses.bsb: I will not presume to speak of anything except what Christ has accomplished through me in leading the Gentiles to obedience by word and deed,

verses.kjv: Through mighty signs and wonders, by the power of the Spirit of God; so that from Jerusalem, and round about unto Illyricum, I have fully preached the gospel of Christ.
verses.bsb: by the power of signs and wonders, and by the power of the Spirit of God. So from Jerusalem all the way around to Illyricum, I have fully proclaimed the gospel of Christ.

verses.kjv: Yea, so have I strived to preach the gospel, not where Christ was named, lest I should build upon another man’s foundation:
verses.bsb: In this way I have aspired to preach the gospel where Christ was not known, so that I would not be building on someone else’s foundation.

verses.kjv: But as it is written, To whom he was not spoken of, they shall see: and they that have not heard shall understand.
verses.bsb: Rather, as it is written:  “Those who were not told about Him will see, and those who have not heard will understand.”

verses.kjv: For which cause also I have been much hindered from coming to you.
verses.bsb: That is why I have often been hindered from coming to you.

verses.kjv: But now having no more place in these parts, and having a great desire these many years to come unto you;
verses.bsb: But now that there are no further opportunities for me in these regions, and since I have longed for many years to visit you,

verses.kjv: Whensoever I take my journey into Spain, I will come to you: for I trust to see you in my journey, and to be brought on my way thitherward by you, if first I be somewhat filled with your company.
verses.bsb: I hope to see you on my way to Spain. And after I have enjoyed your company for a while, you can equip me for my journey.

verses.kjv: But now I go unto Jerusalem to minister unto the saints.
verses.bsb: Now, however, I am on my way to Jerusalem to serve the saints there.

verses.kjv: For it hath pleased them of Macedonia and Achaia to make a certain contribution for the poor saints which are at Jerusalem.
verses.bsb: For Macedonia and Achaia were pleased to make a contribution for the poor among the saints in Jerusalem.

verses.kjv: It hath pleased them verily; and their debtors they are. For if the Gentiles have been made partakers of their spiritual things, their duty is also to minister unto them in carnal things.
verses.bsb: They were pleased to do it, and indeed they owe it to them. For if the Gentiles have shared in their spiritual blessings, they are obligated to minister to them with material blessings.

verses.kjv: When therefore I have performed this, and have sealed to them this fruit, I will come by you into Spain.
verses.bsb: So after I have completed this service and have safely delivered this bounty to them, I will set off to Spain by way of you.

verses.kjv: And I am sure that, when I come unto you, I shall come in the fulness of the blessing of the gospel of Christ.
verses.bsb: I know that when I come to you, I will come in the fullness of the blessing of Christ.

verses.kjv: Now I beseech you, brethren, for the Lord Jesus Christ’s sake, and for the love of the Spirit, that ye strive together with me in your prayers to God for me;
verses.bsb: Now I urge you, brothers, by our Lord Jesus Christ and by the love of the Spirit, to join me in my struggle by praying to God for me.

verses.kjv: That I may be delivered from them that do not believe in Judæa; and that my service which I have for Jerusalem may be accepted of the saints;
verses.bsb: Pray that I may be delivered from the unbelievers in Judea, and that my service in Jerusalem may be acceptable to the saints there,

verses.kjv: That I may come unto you with joy by the will of God, and may with you be refreshed.
verses.bsb: so that by God’s will I may come to you with joy and together with you be refreshed.

verses.kjv: Now the God of peace be with you all. Amen.
verses.bsb: The God of peace be with all of you. Amen.

verses.kjv: I COMMEND unto you Phebe our sister, which is a servant of the church which is at Cenchrea:
verses.bsb: I commend to you our sister Phoebe, a servant of the church in Cenchrea.

verses.kjv: That ye receive her in the Lord, as becometh saints, and that ye assist her in whatsoever business she hath need of you: for she hath been a succourer of many, and of myself also.
verses.bsb: Welcome her in the Lord in a manner worthy of the saints, and assist her with anything she may need from you. For she has been a great help to many people, including me.

verses.kjv: Greet Priscilla and Aquila my helpers in Christ Jesus:
verses.bsb: Greet Prisca and Aquila, my fellow workers in Christ Jesus,

verses.kjv: Who have for my life laid down their own necks: unto whom not only I give thanks, but also all the churches of the Gentiles.
verses.bsb: who have risked their lives for me. Not only I but all the churches of the Gentiles are grateful to them.

verses.kjv: Likewise greet the church that is in their house. Salute my wellbeloved Epænetus, who is the firstfruits of Achaia unto Christ.
verses.bsb: Greet also the church that meets at their house.  Greet my beloved Epenetus, who was the first convert to Christ in the province of Asia.

verses.kjv: Greet Mary, who bestowed much labour on us.
verses.bsb: Greet Mary, who has worked very hard for you.

verses.kjv: Salute Andronicus and Junia, my kinsmen, and my fellowprisoners, who are of note among the apostles, who also were in Christ before me.
verses.bsb: Greet Andronicus and Junia, my fellow countrymen and fellow prisoners. They are outstanding among the apostles, and they were in Christ before I was.

verses.kjv: Greet Amplias my beloved in the Lord.
verses.bsb: Greet Ampliatus, my beloved in the Lord.

verses.kjv: Salute Urbane, our helper in Christ, and Stachys my beloved.
verses.bsb: Greet Urbanus, our fellow worker in Christ, and my beloved Stachys.

verses.kjv: Salute Apelles approved in Christ. Salute them which are of Aristobulus’ household.
verses.bsb: Greet Apelles, who is approved in Christ.  Greet those who belong to the household of Aristobulus.

verses.kjv: Salute Herodion my kinsman. Greet them that be of the household of Narcissus, which are in the Lord.
verses.bsb: Greet Herodion, my fellow countryman.  Greet those from the household of Narcissus who are in the Lord.

verses.kjv: Salute Tryphena and Tryphosa, who labour in the Lord. Salute the beloved Persis, which laboured much in the Lord.
verses.bsb: Greet Tryphena and Tryphosa, women who have worked hard in the Lord.  Greet my beloved Persis, who has worked very hard in the Lord.

verses.kjv: Salute Rufus chosen in the Lord, and his mother and mine.
verses.bsb: Greet Rufus, chosen in the Lord, and his mother, who has been a mother to me as well.

verses.kjv: Salute Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermas, Patrobas, Hermes, and the brethren which are with them.
verses.bsb: Greet Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, Hermas, and the brothers with them.

verses.kjv: Salute Philologus, and Julia, Nereus, and his sister, and Olympas, and all the saints which are with them.
verses.bsb: Greet Philologus and Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas and all the saints with them.

verses.kjv: Salute one another with an holy kiss. The churches of Christ salute you.
verses.bsb: Greet one another with a holy kiss.  All the churches of Christ send you greetings.

verses.kjv: Now I beseech you, brethren, mark them which cause divisions and offences contrary to the doctrine which ye have learned; and avoid them.
verses.bsb: Now I urge you, brothers, to watch out for those who create divisions and obstacles that are contrary to the teaching you have learned. Turn away from them.

verses.kjv: For they that are such serve not our Lord Jesus Christ, but their own belly; and by good words and fair speeches deceive the hearts of the simple.
verses.bsb: For such people are not serving our Lord Christ, but their own appetites. By smooth talk and flattery they deceive the hearts of the naive.

verses.kjv: For your obedience is come abroad unto all men. I am glad therefore on your behalf: but yet I would have you wise unto that which is good, and simple concerning evil.
verses.bsb: Everyone has heard about your obedience, so I rejoice over you. But I want you to be wise about what is good and innocent about what is evil.

verses.kjv: And the God of peace shall bruise Satan under your feet shortly. The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you. Amen.
verses.bsb: The God of peace will soon crush Satan under your feet. The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.

verses.kjv: Timotheus my workfellow, and Lucius, and Jason, and Sosipater, my kinsmen, salute you.
verses.bsb: Timothy, my fellow worker, sends you greetings, as do Lucius, Jason, and Sosipater, my fellow countrymen.

verses.kjv: I Tertius, who wrote this epistle, salute you in the Lord.
verses.bsb: I, Tertius, who wrote down this letter, greet you in the Lord.

verses.kjv: Gaius mine host, and of the whole church, saluteth you. Erastus the chamberlain of the city saluteth you, and Quartus a brother.
verses.bsb: Gaius, who has hosted me and all the church, sends you greetings.  Erastus, the city treasurer, sends you greetings, as does our brother Quartus.

verses.kjv: Now to him that is of power to stablish you according to my gospel, and the preaching of Jesus Christ, according to the revelation of the mystery, which was kept secret since the world began,
verses.bsb: Now to Him who is able to strengthen you by my gospel and by the proclamation of Jesus Christ, according to the revelation of the mystery concealed for ages past

verses.kjv: But now is made manifest, and by the scriptures of the prophets, according to the commandment of the everlasting God, made known to all nations for the obedience of faith:
verses.bsb: but now revealed and made known through the writings of the prophets by the command of the eternal God, in order to lead all nations to the obedience that comes from faith—

verses.kjv: To God only wise, be glory through Jesus Christ for ever. Amen. ¶ Written to the Romans from Corinthus, and sent by Phebe servant of the church at Cenchrea. THE
verses.bsb: to the only wise God be glory forever through Jesus Christ! Amen.
